gleam 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Aggregation Pipeline: Final Herd-Level Results

Source: R/run_aggregation.R
run_aggregation.Rd

This function represents the final step of the Global Livestock Environmental Assessment Model (GLEAM) computational pipeline. It consolidates cohort-level outputs into standardized herd-level totals for reporting. The function (i) scales per-head-per-day variables to cohort totals over the assessment period, (ii) aggregates cohorts to herd level, (iii) allocates emissions to commodities using allocation shares, and (iv) converts CH₄ and N₂O emissions to CO₂-equivalents using a selected GWP-100 option.

run_aggregation(data_cohort, allocation_herd_long, gwp = "AR6")

Arguments

data_cohort

A data.table containing cohort-level data with all calculated variables. Must include:

Feed variables:

dmi (dry matter intake)

Nitrogen balance:

n_intake, n_retention, n_excretion

Production:

output_milk_*, output_meat_*, output_fibre_production

Emissions:

ch4_enteric, ch4_manure_*, direct_n2o_manure_*, indirect_n2o_manure_*

Required grouping columns: herd_id, Animal_short, cohort, assessment_duration, size.

allocation_herd_long

A data.table in long format, typically the output of run_allocation(). Must include columns:

Grouping:

herd_id, Animal_short

Allocation:

commodity_name (e.g., "Meat", "Milk", "Fibre"), allocation_share (numeric, 0-1), allocation_type (character)

Emission source:

variable_name (emission variable names)

gwp

Character scalar specifying the 100-year Global Warming Potential (GWP-100) conversion factors used to express CH₄ and N₂O emissions as CO₂-equivalents. Must be one of:

  • "AR6" (default): IPCC Sixth Assessment Report — CH₄ = 27, N₂O = 273

  • "AR5_excluding_carbon_feedback": IPCC Fifth Assessment Report (excluding climate–carbon feedbacks) — CH₄ = 28, N₂O = 265

  • "AR5_including_carbon_feedback": IPCC Fifth Assessment Report (including climate–carbon feedbacks) — CH₄ = 34, N₂O = 298

  • "AR4": IPCC Fourth Assessment Report — CH₄ = 25, N₂O = 298

Value

A named list containing:

data_cohort

The raw cohort-level input data and results.

results_herd

A data.table in long format with:

  • Allocated emissions (already converted in kgCO2eq)

  • Production variables (milk, meat, fibre)

  • Feed and nitrogen balance variables

  • Standardized variable names and units

  • Commodity classifications and allocation metadata

Columns include: herd_id, Animal_short, cohort (set to "ALL"), variable_type, variable_name, unit, gas, gwp, allocation_type, allocation_share, commodity_type, commodity_name, value_total.

Details

The function follows this workflow:

  1. Reshape to long format. Cohort-level variables are converted from wide to long format using data.table::melt().

  2. Classify variables. Group variables into Emissions, Production, Feed, Nitrogen Balance.

  3. Scale to totals. Variables expressed per head per day are converted to cohort totals over the assessment period using cohort size (size) and assessment_duration.

  4. Aggregate to herd level. Cohort totals are summed to herd totals within each herd_id × Animal_short group.

  5. Merge allocation data: Combine emissions with allocation shares.

  6. Allocate emissions. Emission totals are merged with allocation_herd_long and multiplied by allocation_share to obtain commodity-specific emissions. Non-emission variables are assigned to commodity "ALL" with allocation_share = 1.

  7. Convert to CO₂eq. Allocated CH₄ and N₂O emissions are converted to CO₂eq using the selected GWP-100 option. Results are stored as value_total with unit = "kg CO₂eq".

  8. Standardize output: Rename variables, assign units, and format final results.

References

IPCC (2021). Climate Change 2021: The Physical Science Basis. Contribution of Working Group I to the Sixth Assessment Report of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change. Cambridge University Press.

IPCC (2013). Climate Change 2013: The Physical Science Basis. Contribution of Working Group I to the Fifth Assessment Report of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change. Cambridge University Press.

IPCC (2007). Climate Change 2007: The Physical Science Basis. Contribution of Working Group I to the Fourth Assessment Report of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change. Cambridge University Press.

Contents

Developed by Ahmed Jou, Yassine Elaouni, Dominik Wisser, Lydia Lanzoni, Giuseppe Tempio, Federal Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Regional Identity of Germany BMELH.

Site built with pkgdown 2.1.3.
